evals.prompt: When someone replies to my Mailshake campaign, I want to create a task in Salesforce and get a Slack notification.
evals.expected_output: Integration design using Mailshake reply event with Salesforce task creation and Slack notification

assertions.name: webhook_or_zapier
assertions.description: Recommends webhook or Zapier approach using Mailshake's reply event

assertions.name: mailshake_reply_event
assertions.description: References Mailshake's Reply webhook event or Zapier trigger

assertions.name: multi_step
assertions.description: Designs a multi-step workflow that handles both Salesforce task creation and Slack notification

evals.prompt: I need to sync all Salesloft activity to HubSpot so our marketing team can see sales touchpoints.
evals.expected_output: Activity sync design with direction, field mapping, and native integration recommendation

assertions.name: native_check
assertions.description: Checks for native Salesloft-HubSpot integration before recommending custom solutions

assertions.name: sync_direction
assertions.description: Defines clear sync direction (Salesloft → HubSpot, one-way for activity data)

assertions.name: field_mapping
assertions.description: Addresses field mapping between Salesloft activities and HubSpot engagement properties

evals.prompt: When a lead hits MQL status in HubSpot, I want to auto-enroll them in an Apollo sequence.
evals.expected_output: MQL-triggered enrollment workflow with trigger definition, API/Zapier implementation, and error handling

assertions.name: trigger_definition
assertions.description: Defines the trigger: HubSpot lifecycle stage change to MQL

assertions.name: implementation_options
assertions.description: Recommends Zapier or API-based approach for HubSpot → Apollo enrollment

assertions.name: error_handling
assertions.description: Addresses error scenarios — duplicate contacts, sequence capacity, enrollment failures
